channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign after the issue lingered on the margins for months.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mormon faith pushes ahead with global temple building boom despite cool reception in Las Vegas
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A historic building boom of big, bright Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints temples — beacons to the faithful with steeples pointing heavenward around the world — is meeting resistance in some parts of the U.S., including one place not really known for moderation.
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Human connections bring hope in North Carolina after devastation of Helene
item.description: BLACK MOUNTAIN, N.C. (AP) — Sarah Vekasi is a potter who runs a store in Black Mountain, North Carolina, called Sarah Sunshine Pottery, named after her normally bubbly personality. But these days she’s struggling with the trauma of Hurricane Helene and uncertainty about the future of her business.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, ERIK VERDUZCO and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Haunted by 2016, some Michigan Democrats worry that Harris remains ill-defined in swing state
item.description: EASTPOINTE, Mich. (AP) — Bill Clinton stood with Michigan Democratic Rep. Debbie Dingell outside a suburban Detroit grocery store two days before the 2016 election when it was clear to both that Hillary Clinton would lose the state.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Alleged Diddy victim says cops won't turn over 2018 rape report
item.description: Attorney Ariel Mitchell-Kidd said she's "floored" and that "it's apparent that some type of cover-up is going on.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Helene's flooding swept away 11 workers at a Tennessee factory. Now the state is investigating
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee state authorities said Wednesday they were investigating the company that owns a plastics factory where 11 workers were swept away by cataclysmic flooding unleashed by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Seattle judge releases accused killer 27 hours after 65-year-old military veteran stabbed to death
item.description: A judge in Washington state released a 19-year-old man accused of stabbing a 65-year-old handyman and veteran to death at a home in Renton.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon is convicted of threatening former Speaker McCarthy
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A Montana man was convicted Wednesday of threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy after becoming upset that the government had not shot down a Chinese spy balloon that floated over his home city.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shoppers are panic-buying toilet paper amid port strike, but experts say there’s no need
item.description: “Hope you have some toilet paper stock piled. It’s started. Sam’s and Costco are depleted,” one user said on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYPD officer accused in NJ road rage shooting was a 'ticking timebomb': Lawsuit
item.description: “This case is a product of the way the police department has conducted itself over the years and this is the tragedy that ultimately was going to happen,” the family’s lawyer said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors say they may bring additional charges against New York City mayor and indict others
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors said Wednesday they are pursuing “several related investigations” that could lead to additional corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams and indictments for people in his orbit.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Helene's victims include first responders who died helping others
item.description: Falling trees and raging floods from Hurricane Helene killed more than 150 people when it struck Florida late last week and then plowed through the Southeast as one of the deadliest storms in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal, state officials announce arrest of 68 members of neo-Nazi group in Los Angeles
item.description: A total of 68 people were indicted as part of the “Peckerwood gang,” Estrada confirmed. Of the 68, a total of 42 are in custody.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: After employees swept away in Helene flooding, Tennessee factory under investigation
item.description: Several employees have accused the company of not letting them leave soon enough as flooding set in on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tribes celebrate the end of the largest dam removal project in US history
item.description: The largest dam removal project in U.S. history was completed Wednesday, marking a major victory for tribes in the region who fought for decades to free hundreds of miles of the Klamath River near the California-Oregon border.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Parents spot kids in 7-Eleven robbery footage, turn them in to police
item.description: In each instance, the crew rolled up to the stores on bicycles during the evening hours and stole items, damaged property and left without paying, police said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man sentenced to 6 months in prison for effort to create giant sheep hybrids
item.description: A Montana man was sentenced to six months in prison for crimes committed during his effort to create a giant sheep hybrid for captive hunting, the Department of Justice (DOJ) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KTLA 5

item.title: Kentucky sheriff seen in footage shooting at judge in shocking preliminary hearing
item.description: Video shown at the preliminary hearing for a former Kentucky sheriff, accused of killing a district judge, allegedly showed the deadly shooting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Federal prosecutors file new indictment against ex-Louisville police officers
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Federal prosecutors filed a new indictment Tuesday against two former Louisville officers accused of falsifying a warrant that led police to Breonna Taylor’s door before they fatally shot her.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets’ for the Halloween season
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween. The Boo Buckets, a customer-favorite which doubles as a trick-or-treating bucket, comes with Happy Meals. The company will introduce a blue bucket this year, replacing last year’s purple one.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain fell on the South thanks to Helene and other storms
item.description: That's enough to fill the Dallas Cowboys' stadium 51,000 times, or 60 million Olympic-size swimming pools.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, ABC 4

item.title: Startling video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Military officials have released new video of a startling encounter between a Russian fighter jet flying near Alaska and a U.S. Air Force F-16 sent to intercept it. In the video released Monday, the Russian plane comes from behind the camera and swoops by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baseball legend Pete Rose dies at 83
item.description: Pete Rose, Major League Baseball’s all-time hits leader who endured a lifetime ban for betting on the sport, has died at age 83, his agent Jeff Lenkov confirmed to KTLA 5 News. According to TMZ, Rose died at his home in Las Vegas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: Helene's death toll rises to more than 130 as supplies are rushed to areas in need
item.description: A crisis unfolded in Asheville, North Carolina, as officials pledged to get more water, food and other supplies to flood-stricken areas without power and cellular service Monday, days after Hurricane Helene ripped across the U.S. Southeast. The death toll from the storm surpassed 130.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams accepted harmless 'courtesies,' not bribes, his lawyer says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams launched a legal attack on the federal corruption case against him Monday, with his attorney asking a judge to toss out bribery charges and then holding a combative news conference accusing prosecutors of ethical lapses.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: When do hurricane names get retired?
item.description: With nearly two months to go until the end of what was predicted to be a "hyperactive" hurricane season, many residents along Florida's Gulf Coast and southeastern states are grappling with the damage and destruction left in the wake of the latest hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, Fox 5

item.title: Gavin Creel, Tony Award-winning veteran of Broadway musicals, dies at 48
item.description: Creel's publicist said he died at his home in Manhattan of metastatic melanotic peripheral nerve sheath sarcoma.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Fox 5

item.title: It's a 'very difficult time' for U.S. Jews as High Holy Days and Oct. 7 anniversary coincide
item.description: Known as “The Days of Awe,” Judaism’s High Holy Days — which begin on Wednesday — annually provide an emotional mix of celebration, introspection and atonement for Jews around the world.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Basketball Hall of Famer Dikembe Mutombo dies at 58
item.description: Mutombo starred in the NBA for 18 seasons and is considered to be one of the greatest defensive players in the history of the sport.
item.dc:creator: Brayden Stamps, ABC 4

item.title: Death toll rises after Helene slams the South
item.description: Even as Helene was downgraded from a powerful Category 4 hurricane to a tropical storm, it continued to dump heavy rain as it blew through the South Friday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9
